column1: Landing page examples of successful brands for inspiration

column2:
AICPBSAWN
1. Attention – Biggest benefit, biggest problem you can solve, USP
2. Interest – Reason why they should be interested in what you have to say
3. Credibility – Reason why they should believe you
4. Prove – Prove what you are claiming is true
5. Benefits – List them all (use bullets)
6. Scarcity – Create scarcity
7. Action – Tell them precisely what to do
8. Warn – What will happen if they don’t take action
9. Now – Motivate them to take action now

This new funnel structure has worked at a 5-1 ROAS to cold traffic WITHOUT a high ticket back end (high ticket makes the numbers silly).

If you have tried low ticket with an upsell flow and…

1) it didn’t feel right to you and you’re not sure why or

2) couldn’t get it to work because there were too many moving parts to optimize and you’re not a scientist.

3) you just want a simpler way of doing things that hopefully sacrifices nothing but opens up significant sales and marketing leverage…

This might he’p ya out a bit.

Would encourage you to encourage yourself to position your thinking cap at a different angle today, because this will require a shift for it to land properly.

It’s kind opposite the norm, but Alen tells me to post it anyways.

To me, the big problem with popular LOW ticket sales processes is that it’s NOT an ideal human interaction.

It predictably manipulates human nature but disregards the human.

We are trained as marketers to get a direct response quickly by using human nature to our advantage and it works great (but always for a minority percentage).

That percentage is often a predictable metric.

Means that when you hit standard metrics, you’re good. Scale it up because it can’t get any better than that, right?

BUT the downside is that often times despite our best efforts, the human behind the metrics is left feeling violated and threatened.

Mostly because the “buyer’s remorse” style feelings don’t show up until AFTER the response (the purchase).

Not good for long term relationship building, which is what stable businesses (especially during rough economic times) should be based on.

This is why the typical metrics we see are stuck, capped, and are rarely beaten.

What would happen to the metrics if we solved for that downside simultaneously?

Could we blow the lid off maybe?

Years ago I didn’t see very many people trying to solve for that (there’s more of us now though).

That’s what I’ve attempted to do (but this isn’t about me, rest of backstory is below).

I’m certain there’s more than one way to solve for that and I can’t say that my way will work for everyone but it worked for me in one of the hardest industries in the world: low ticket personal development.

And it has worked incredibly well for clients.

So let me outline the model.

Since this is so different there’s a lot to explain but I’m tryna keep this short and sweet and simple.

What I won’t go through is all the current problems with low and high ticket funnels.

You know what they are because you experience them all day.

To me, this new funnel structure is a timely response to current market conditions and it solves a bunch of problems all at once.

So I’ll just tell you what it is, then you be the judge.

WHAT IT IS:

It’s called a Deposit Funnel™

A simplified, alternative way to run a high-converting low-ticket front end that still liquidates ad spend very well while maximizing LTV without having to repair your reputation or mess with upsell/downsell/bumps or aggressive outbound sales tactics.

HOW IT WORKS:

Read the pic to get an idea of how to position this kind of an offer.

The offer I mention in that post does not exist (yet). It's purely an example.

Basics…
I use a one-step one-time DEPOSIT instead of a PURCHASE plus upsells. Example: $95 deposit VS $7 product + upsells.
Instead of delivering a product/program/training we package it all into an EXPERIENCE that produces single quantifiable result quickly… and we STOP selling anything that doesn’t directly contribute to the result.
Optimize for the transformation (aka a meaningful result) instead of the transaction and we get more and bigger transactions.
You may have heard of deposits before.

I’ve put some twists on it that are fun and have optimized to create killer clients on the backend.

But they must be used only when you can bank on a specific result being created by the experience you provide.

Your use case will differ, but my personal development funnel called the Breakthrough Challenge works like this…

Optin ==> 14,000 word letter ==> $95 Deposit (NO UPSELLS) ==> 21-day email-based transformational experience ==> $995 offer (minus $95 deposit + points they accrued for an average of $704).

I don’t currently offer high ticket coaching openly for this offer BUT clients reach out to me asking for it.

The experience you provide them can be delivered in any way you want. The only thing that is important is that it delivers the intended result predictably.

For me, I use email as the backbone for a bunch of different reasons.

Yours could be 5, 7, 14, 21 days, however long is needed.

My 21-day experience has built in accountability and gamification. It’s designed to capture and keep their attention. And it does.

My offer at the end is for an “info product” they can buy but you can have them book a call or message and do a Travis Sago style tap or two…

But you can put anything there… membership, in person event, high ticket coaching/consulting, a service, whatever…

The key: the upsell has to be a continuation of what they’ve started. Meaning, the experience you provide is ONE STEP in a larger journey.

When they finish the experience and have the result in hand, they then have a few options…
Get their deposit back.
Leave the deposit as a tip.
Credit the deposit towards the next thing.
I have automated all of this, including the automatic discount of the points the accrued.

If they don’t make it to the end (traditionally, less than 10% flunk out)… then their deposit is NOT kept. You can donate it to charity if you want. But the point is that we don’t want them to feel like we’re incentivized to flunk them out of the experience.

Might think it produces terrible clients.

I’ve never had a bad client doing this. Not once.

WHY IT WORKS:

Well, I’m sure you can figure out why this works so I don’t need to wax philosophical.

Yes there are nuances, but the building blocks are remarkably simple.

There’s only three core pieces you have to nail.

Transform + Transfix + Transact.

In that order.

Not sure what to call that framework other than T3 Protocol or something.

It ticks all the boxes of a benevolent way of selling.

To me the coolest part is that a DEPOSIT doesn’t necessarily feel like a transaction but it performs the function beautifully. Still liquidates ad spend. Stripe doesn’t know any different, but the prospect can feel the difference.

A deposit is open ended. It optimizes both you and them to show up and deliver the result. I love it. Also, inherently in the idea of a deposit is the fact that we put down a deposit like a down payment, it’s natural to credit it forward. We can be open and honest with exactly what we’re doing to or with them.

Using a deposit humanizes the offer, removes risk of the “buyer’s remorse” downside while quickly creating deep trust and connection AND most importantly a RESULT that matters to them.

There are LOADS of benefits for doing this. Way too many to list here.

It’s a massively powerful force multiplier for a high ticket coaching/consulting business.

But here are a few I like…

SHORT TERM BENEFITS:

Liquidates ad spend pretty well because you can charge more for a deposit (good AOV) and conversion is generally higher (CAC goes down).

Most importantly… by delaying the transaction until AFTER a clear result is delivered (remember a deposit doesn’t feel like a transaction) you are actually speeding up the high ticket sales cycle without the need to chase down sales with high pressure tactics that violate the relationship before you even have one.

It optimizes the psychology to where the participant is ready to buy SOONER.

Email response rates are incredible and they stay that way.

LONG TERM BENEFITS:

This acts as the centerpiece to your entire operation. Instead of having to create 17 funnels, just have one. It’s simple.

Our market is full of noise. What it is empty of is results. This will separate you from your market because you deliver meaningful results that advance the sale. Not just hype or hollow promises or saying whatever needs to be said to get the sale.

So long as you can deliver on the results, you develop an unbeatable reputation. Happy clients the stick around and happily share their experience. Reputation that soars.

Simply put: LTV is niiiiiice.

METRICS

This is where you might throw rocks. So many of you are far smarter than me. I’m no scientist. In fact, the only way I was able to figure this stuff out was because I wasn’t optimizing initially for transactions at all. I had to figure out how to drive transformations predictably, that’s all I cared about. That part came pretty easy for me.

Turns out it was far more difficult than I had thought to get the transactions right. Turns out the transformations were tooooo good. I was creating transformations but nobody bought on the back end.

Took YEARS to figure out why. When I did, people started to buy at a seemingly high rate and I wasn’t even tracking the metrics. Dumb right?

The first time I dug into my CRM and calculated the basic metrics like LTV and sales conversion etc, my jaw hit the floor.

21-DAY COMPLETION: 85-96%

REFUNDS: 6% (even though the offer is a refund).

CONVERSION: Lead to sale conversion rate: 9% to 63% depending on traffic source. Cold traffic was 42% last time I tested it.

LTV: $612 per participant in the deposit funnel.

EMAIL: A steady 90%+ opens and clicks that doesn’t drop off after welcome email.

NOTE: I am working on running this AT SCALE. Which is not something I can confidently say I’ve done. So I’m restructuring a few things to operate smoothly with thousands instead of hundreds of participants.

Example BETA client metrics for a high ticket offer, also in personal development:

Deposit: $47

Experience: 14-days.

Lead (participant) to call conversion: 80%

Show up rate: 100%

LTV: $2050 per participant.

WARNING: There is a dark side to this.

IF you can’t produce a result, you’ll be exposed and there’s no way to ethically pull this off. So start there if that’s you.

Does require that you know how to produce a result for your people. This post is not about that. If you are an expert but are not sure how to create real life results, this will still help.

Backstory:

My problem has always been I’m REALLY strong at marketing and selling BUT I’m hyper-aware of that downside. Cripplingly aware of it.

Have been since 2004 when I started in high ticket coaching/consulting space.

And I’ve spent my entire career trying to figure out an optimal way of selling without that downside.

Was told it wasn’t possible by dozens of friends and peers and mentors.

I would hire incredible coaches to learn a way of selling and funneling only to end up doing things opposite. Stubborn.

Harlan Kilstein can attest to this. I’m probably his worst client ever, but I love that man.

Ate sh*t for years trying to figure this out… watching all my friends crush it doing things the way they’ve always been done.

Until that way of doing things started to slow down.

And now it’s not working nearly as well and people are scrambling because it’s all they know. Sell harder and faster has been the answer.

I’ve found the opposite is true.

You can sell more and faster by DELAYING the sale for as long as possible instead of going for the immediate home run.

There you have it.

What’s next?

Hit me with all your what ifs and yeah buts please. I am intended to teach all this stuff properly when the time is right.

I shared it for the first time publicy at Ian Stanley's email event last year and I wasn't prepared for the response. Been a whirlwind since.

Would love to pressure test this with all the smart people in this room.

I’ve been so far down this rabbit hole for years that sometimes I can’t see the simple any more.

DISCLAIMER: wrote this post pretty quick, bound to be some typos and poorly written thingies. It's such a new concept I'm still trying to figure out how to explain it properl

column1: Landing page Trick to increase AOV
column2:
The universe is 13.6 billion years old. And humans have only been around for 2 million years.
If the age of the universe was a 24 hour day - we just rolled onto the scene at 11:59:51 PM.
In other words…
The human brain has had less than 10 cosmic seconds to develop since we were living in caves dancing the unga bunga and gnawing on bones.
That’s not very long.
Which is why so many of the evolutionary switches that kept us alive back then still have so much influence over us.
As a marketer, it’s important you understand that.
In fact, when you get down to it - flipping those switches is really all that we do.
And one of the most powerful switches you can flip? Herd mentality.
Back in the day, following the crowd helped us find food, warmth, shelter, and safety in numbers.
There are precisely 4 bajillion different ways it manifests in the modern world…
And you can use one of those ways to multiply your sales.
It’s pretty straightforward:
Whenever you include a link to a product in an email, add a few words before it. Something like:
“Most people order 3.”
That’s it. Maybe justify it with something like “...because we constantly run out of stock.”
But that’s enough. It flips that “follow the herd” switch for a lot of people and gets their lizard brain thinking:
“Golly, I’d like to have this shiny new thing that solves my current problem. And I’d like to not have this problem again. If everyone else is ordering 3, maybe I should, too.”
It doesn’t complicate the sale. It doesn’t trigger any objections.
At worst, they ignore it.
At best, you just convinced them to double, triple, maybe even quadruple their order.
Fiendish? Maybe. Manipulative? Perhaps.
But you can’t deny its effectiveness.
Try it out in your next broadcast email and see for yourself!

Now, what this does is...say you have a 5 minute timer, but you make it tick 30% FASTER, what you REALLY have is a 3.5 minute timer…

...Or if you have a 3 minute timer and you make it tick 30% SLOWER, now you have a 4 minute timer.

The human body has a built in circadian rhythm that tracks time.
You know how you can just wake up when you tell yourself you have to wake up at that time? That’s how it works.

When this rhythm is out of alignment the human body creates anxiety.
Slow Fast timers cause this to get out of balance and thus that’s why they work.

So...where do you use them?

From yesterdays post on pre-pop events…

...If you do a TIMER on a pre-pop, you put a SLOWER ticking timer…
...So you say “You’re going to get access in 5 minutes”, and the timer ticks slow, really it’s 8 minutes, so now you got them to watch 8 MORE minutes of your VSL…

...Than they goto the checkout page, and they have “5 minutes to buy” because of the timer, but it’s really 3 minutes...
...Are you starting to get it now? Good! Now, you have an ability to distort time itself on your offers…
...And one of the most powerful tricks that NO ONE ever will figure out — unless you specifically told them you’re doing it.

column1: Sub-testimonials. how to add testimonials without having them
column2:
What comes first…chicken or the egg? That’s the age-old question when it comes to building offers…
…After all, you need testimonials to sell your product, but you also need to sell your product in order to get testimonials.
So what do you do? Well, you have a few options…
…You can sell the product without them, add them later, give them away for free in exchange for testimonials, and so on and on...
…If you’ve spent any time in direct response, I’m sure you know the “tricks of the trade”.
And they all work and get the job done…
…The thing is, as most markets mature and as everyone uses testimonials as standard practice, they lose their effectiveness as a whole...
…Customers just tune them out because there is an over-saturation of claims in the marketplace, and traditional testimonials do nothing but saturate them even more.
And as such, what you will notice is that not having them on the page can result in higher conversion rates.
The reason for this is simple…
… Testimonials matter because they back up the claims presented in the copy.
…The problem is, they can and often are fabricated and so the customer only responds to certain types of testimonials.
The 4 types of testimonials the customer responds to are:
1. Is the person in the testimonial just like them age, gender, look, career, circumstances, etc…?
2. Do they know the person personally?
3. Do they KNOW OF the person? Meaning are they an authority or famous in the niche?
4. Does the person have authority in the market they’re in, a thought leader, etc..…
Getting the #1 and #2…chances are slim.
Getting #3 is a little easier as you can get an influencer or two to give you a testimonial, but you can’t get that many, so that’s not bad.
Except there is nothing worse than having 1-3 testimonials, which basically subcommittees that only 1-3 people used it and got results and no one else.
So the subcommission is that either only 1-3 people got results or no one is buying it. Meh, not good.
This leaves us with #4, does this person have authority in the niche?
Well, now, this is interesting and allows us to create what I call Sub-Testimonials.

Sub, because they subcommittee, and sub because they substitute traditional testimonials.
They’re testimonials about the PARTS of the product, not the product in itself.
So say you have a supplement that has 8 ingredients. You could quote what some authority or researcher has said about that particular ingredient as a backup to your claim so long as you link to the source where the customer can do further diligence on it.
And thus, they are testimonials, but of a different kind.
There are many ways you can do this…
…One way is to go to news.google.com and type in “Ingredient,” and search. What you will find are pages and pages of articles that are positively talking about the subcomponent of your product or service.
Most people use PubMed for this, except are linking the citations at the bottom.
It’s not much different, except you a direct quote and link and say, for example:
Here’s what scientists at acme university had to say about their findings about ingredient x.
“In a study in 2015, Turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation by x%, by the mechanism of XXXX without YYYY”.
That’s an oversimplified example, but the core difference is that it is not attached to any person as the person getting results, so there is no “I” in there.
Once framed as testimonials, a testimony, but of a different kind, they become powerful tools in your copy arsenal that not only boost conversions but are also compliant, as the general rule for testimonials is that they should be the sum average that the customer can expect to get.
Getting a “sum average” with a traditional testimonial is tough, but with this…as it does not directly imply the results the person got, but rather a 3rd party “test” of sorts, it’s different.
Here’s the process
1. Goto news.google.com
2. Search for, say, “turmeric benefits.”
3. 3rd article is by Daily Mail which reads
4. "The golden key: Could the world's most scientifically studied turmeric supplement help unlock your joint health?”
5. Here is how a pseudo testimonial would look like
6. “Consuming a diet rich in fruits, dark leafy vegetables, and oily fish brings helpful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits. A growing number of people are also turning to turmeric-based products and nutritional supplements for extra support.” Daily Mail, April 28th, 2023 (https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-11969481/Key-joint-health-People-unlock-benefits-worlds-studied-turmeric-supplement.html)
7. Of course, you put it in a testimonial box, etc…
Using this process, now you’ve just gone from not having any testimonials, having to send products out and deal with all that, to having a process to having an infinite amount of testimonials that are legit, better, and easy to get.
Also, it would be best if you had a disclaimer on the page that any of these brands do not endorse your product, etc...as it could lead to extrapolation of claims, so check with a lawyer in your final compliance call and state that you are merely using it for research purposes to explain how your product works.

As always, DO NOT ever, under any circumstances, makeup or fabricate testimonials, and use the ones you can substantiate.
- Alen

The Dishonest Bump / Upsell - An Easy End of Funnel AOV Booster

Sent this out to the list a week ago but forgot to post it here, so here it is. 

This is an easy upsell you can add to the end of your funnel that will boost up your AOV.

It costs you nothing to add, and it can be done in just a few minutes.

You know, there are a lot of dishonest people out there...

...And one of the ways dishonesty emerges in your business is

when you send your customer something, and someone steals it 
just before they get it.

Some people call these types "porch pirates", and if you use the
Next Door App (which you should btw), what you'll notice is that
it happens all too often, and there is a huge top-of-mind awareness around this as the local news tends to cover it.

Mostly used for where you send something out, so this is great for supplement and eCom offers.

Here's the swipe (play with the price for your market $4.95 - $9.95 range is the sweet spot):

---------------------------------------------------------

"40% Of Americans Are Porch Pirate Victims!"

DON'T BE THE NEXT VICTIM OF PORCH PIRATES
AND MAKE SURE YOU GET YOUR ORDER...

[Picture of a Porch Pirate]

NO QUESTIONS ASKED, ORDER REPLACEMENT FOR ONLY $9.95

If your package is ever lost, stolen, or damaged in transit.  Our "Porch Pirates Protection" insurance is an extra layer of security and peace of mind.

Just add it to your order, and it will allow us to ship you a replacement at no extra cost to you, no questions asked.

[Yes Protect My Order Against Loss & Theft]

---------------------------------------------------------

That's it, just copy and paste that at the end of your funnel, and you're set, OR you can use it as a bump.

- Alen Sultanic

p.s. Hit the comment box for love, questions, algo bump or appreciation.

Breaking Down a Landing Page Converting Cold Traffic At 10% That I Recently Launched

This one's directly for the ecommerce folks, but the lessons in here are useful for anyone in any industry, with any funnel. 



*(I also attached the full image of the landing page, for those who want to see the entire thing in one spot.)



I just launched this landing page for zerotaboos, and it increased their conversion rates by nearly 1000%. They went from a 0.01% conversion rate to nearly 10%.



Before, they couldn't get their ads to turn a profit. A common problem. We made some tweaks that laid the foundation for a super-profitable campaign as we scale.



I'm going to walk you through how we designed the upper parts of the page (the parts that matter most) so you can easily turn more of your traffic into paying customers.



THE BREAKDOWN - HERO SECTION

I attached a photo of the beginning of the page, also called the "hero" section.



It's the most important part of the page because it's the part everyone sees. Every part of the page below this depends on how well this part made the viewer want to continue reading.



First things first, people expect to see a rating, so it should be the first thing you put on the page. As buyers, we care about what other people think because it helps us know whether we should trust the product or not. Most people would rather not be the first to try a product.



HEADLINE

Next: The headline of the page is the second focus. This part of the page should simply tell the viewer why they should care about the product. Brands get this wrong all the time, but it's important to get right. Without doing this the right way, brands end up sounding self-centered and not giving visitors a reason to buy.



SUBHEADLINE

For the subhead, we included light product details. People don't care that much yet, but this is the time to let people know an overview of some more details about the benefit and how your product brings it.



OVERVIEW POINTS

Next, see those checkmark-bullet-point-things below the subhead? I call those overview points. I include 3-4 benefits or features of the product here. Typically, make sure they're aligned with what people appreciate about your product or points that help people understand how your product works.



SOCIAL PROOF

In the next section (where you'll see a review), we usually place press mentions: so, the CNNs, the ABCs, the Good Housekeepings, etc. Publications. Professional social proof.



However, it's not always the most convincing (especially with younger audiences) as people know these publications can be paid off.



If you don't have them, use a review as we did here. Pick one that really illustrates what they appreciated about your product. "great product, guys!" might be a nice review to have, but it won't cut it on your landing page.



Next, we'll tackle one of the most important pieces of the page—the Problem Section.



Without it, most landing pages will be useless. With it, you create customers that won't even touch a competitor's product.



Let me know if this was useful! I'd love to jump into more of these and help more folks here.

column1: YT funnel for a Viagra Replacement Product

column2:
Cool offer optimization witchcraft magic 🧙🏻 for your upsell take-rates...As much as 832%...[FREE SWIPE BELOW]

So...
Recently got hired to increase AOV on an offer — And the biggest needle mover to do that is to increase conversions on upsell 1 since it has high price elasticity (aka you can charge more for it) -shout out to Alen Sultanic for beating that into my head over the last few years....
So here's what I did to get a 832% lift on an upsell 1 (text sales letter) all by using a simple headline tweak...

---------------------------------------------------------------
So as I read the letter for the control, I realize their were FREE SAMPLES of the product WAY BELOW ON the page...
BUT...
As we know, most people can't be bothered to scroll down or even consume your upsell messaging after making their first purchase...

Hence why they just BOUNCE...
So you got to give them a reason to stay and scroll down...
So all I did was advertise in the headline that Free Samples were below... (THESE SAMPLES WERE FOR AN ONLINE COURSE AND JUST SHOWED MINI PREVIEWS AND CLIPS FROM IT -
And boom!
Now bounce rates are lower, and people are consuming the sales message....
...Seeing all the pain-points and outcomes again as they do so...
And getting to the samples, playing them, and then BUYING it! (this isolates attention = more sales as alen always)
I won't reveal the offer because of confidentiality...
But here's the original OTO 1 headline vs the variant (which will soon be the new control as soon as we finish getting enough data)
------------------------------------------------
Control:
Hey [guru Here - I'd Love To [help you get outcome in the Next X Days ... Here's How I Can Do That For You - With Very Little Of Your Involvement!
And It Will Take Only 10 Minutes A Day
... Read To Learn More …
Variant (soon to be control):
WAIT! Your Order is NOT complete! -
Please Read This Short But Important Letter And Click At The Bottom To Complete Your Order
Hey [guru] Here - I'd Love To help you get outcome in the next X days] ... Here's How I Can Do That For You - With Very Little Of Your Involvement! - In Only 10 Minutes A Day!
[Free Samples Below!]
----------------------------

RESULT 832% Lift And Test Is Halfway Over...But these high numbers should hold whenever everything is said and done

---------------------------
There you have it - BIG takeaway here is that your headline has the power to make them stay - but you can't bury your best sales messaging way below the page.
You got to bring those up higher (or at least nod to them) in the headline real-estate area...
AND IF YOU HAVE A WAY TO SHOW THEM A PREVIEW OF THE PRODUCT, YOU'RE GOING TO GET LESS BOUNCE RATES/MASSIVE LIFTS

(You don't have to give away the whole enchilada — Just find a way to give them a taste...A sample...
P.S.
Remember to remind them that their order is still being process and that they should move through the upsell page to complete their oder.
The original version didn't have that part and the upsell just kind of seem to come out of nowhere and caused friction in the sales process....

column1: On hiding the pricing vs not hiding the pricing
column2:
If your product is high quality and delivers results, then by all means, show the whole world your price as your product can back it up. You never see Apple or any other high-quality product-based company "hide the price". Instead, they're proud of it.
The only time people tend to hide the price is when they're afraid that the customer won't really like the product.
Once you change your assumption that the product in itself is top-notch, then your energy in terms of being up front about the price will become an advantage instead of a disadvantage.
So the price is ________ because ______________.

column1:

column2:
The Hidden Architecture of Quiz Funnels — Here's how to create scalable quiz funnels in any market or niche (even if you've never done it before)



Morning, afternoon, or good evening, depending on what part of the world you’re reading this post from...

...It’s been quite a while since I dropped a long-form banger here and for a good reason — creating the best marketers in the game, running the biggest and best mastermind building an industry-changing platform and secretly building 3 other game-changing projects takes a lot of time and energy...

...But thankfully I have an incredible team to free me up a bit so I’m back at it.

So let’s put aside the humble brags and focus on what matters most right now, and that’s helping you master the art of marketing.

Today, I’m going to take you behind the scenes of something that’s been right in front of you all along and yet you’ve never seen it.

This is something I taught in Fast Forward about 10-12 months ago, and in NHB+ a few months ago, and I’ve also used this exact process to create one of the biggest health back ends for ClickBank weight loss offers that’s booking a ridiculous amount of calls and selling high-ticket (don’t ask me what the link is because the last thing the person wants is for you to funnel hack him and copy what I teach here).

...It’s also been used in dating to get CPAs at $2-3, biz-op, manifestation niche, home services, and pretty much anything you can imagine. 

It works in every single niche, not because it’s a marketing “strategy” or “tactic”, but because it works on deep human psychology, and since humans are the only ones interacting with what we do online, it’s safe to say that it can work in just about any niche :).

So let’s talk about quiz funnels. How they work, what makes them work, what everyone else is doing wrong and what you can do to dominate the quiz funnel game. 

Let’s talk about the hidden architecture of quiz funnels.

Now, fair warning...this is a 3-hour in-depth step-by-step call I did condense into a single post, so A LOT of minor details have been left out and only the major retained to save you some reading time. 

If you’re in NHB+ or FF, watch the call to get those details.

Let's continue...

When it comes to quiz funnels, what everyone does is just throw up an ad, and that ad goes to a quiz and that quiz just asks a bunch of questions.

The questions often have ZERO thought, ZERO purpose, and ZERO function.

They merely identify the customer based on some data point such as income or whatever so they can do something with that data later on the back end, namely phone sales.

The problem of the traditional quiz model is twofold:


1. They only use identifier questions and that causes the loss of intent as they go through the quiz funnel and the deeper they go, the less interested they are in buying it. The only way to offset that loss of intent is with ever-higher prices. You can see this by low quiz completion rates. People just bounce out.

2. Hence why most quiz funnels lead to some type of book a call that sells a thing for a bazillion dollars. 

3. It catches a lot of customers with curiosity up front, yet it does not do anything to convert that curiosity to intent and without intent it’s harder to make a sale.



The reason for the above is that captures of quiz funnels tends to lead them into an “information-seeking state” but it does not move them to a problem state and then a solution state.



In order for someone to buy, they have to be in a SOLUTION state, not a PROBLEM state. 



The thing is, the way you get to a SOLUTION state is VIA a problem state.



So a typical quiz funnel will use the model of:



INFORMATION SEEKING STATE -> PITCH



So you’re basically catching a lot of fish, only to keep a few at the end, kinda of pointless tbh.



When it comes to any type of offer, every single word, action, move, or element on the page has to have a purpose and a FUNCTION and its job is to affect the conversion via change work.



Then the question becomes, what affects change and how?



As a quiz funnel is nothing but a series of questions, that means that the nature of those questions has to cause the change, hence the use of functional questions.



So what SHOULD be done is that there should be the creation of intent via functional questions as they’re going through the quiz process.



This concept of a “functional questions” is the difference that makes the difference here.



Because you see, what most quiz funnels do is they “IDENTIFY THE CUSTOMER”, but what’s different about this model is it actually “CREATES THE CUSTOMER”.



Now, it’s getting interesting and you’re about to see some real magic in the game.



Let dig into this a little deeper, because it’s REALLY REALLY important.



If you look around, everyone wants to do this online thing, like legit EVERYONE, which means that as time goes on, the competition as a whole is increasing in every single niche.



We have proxy data here to prove this point, and that’s the fact that the cost of ads is ever-increasing.



The only reason the cost of ads is increasing is if there is more demand on ads, and the only way we can have more demand for ads is if more people are bidding for those ads.



Hence, more and more people want to play this game we play.



And with that in mind, as everyone out there wants to do this online thing...



...That means everyone out there is fishing for customers who are ready to buy, yet very few are getting them to buy.



Meaning, let's say...you have 1,000,000 people in a market. 



At any given moment, only about 1-3% of that audience is “ready to buy”, so out of say a million potential customers you’ll have 10,000-30,000 people in total who are “sellable”, and 970,000-990,000 sitting on the fence watching everything, neither here nor there, who are “not sellable”.



So you have EVERYONE competing for the same 10,000 - 30,000 “sellable” people.



The sellable people are the ones who are in a solution-seeking state, the rest are either in a problem state or pre-problem state.



Whoever knows how to sell to pre-problem or problem-state customers and flip them over INTO solution-state customers owns the market because they can use those economics to acquire even more solution-state customers.



Those that can do this can literally 10-100x their audiences and market size, and dare I say...”scale”.



Real scale.



That’s why it’s so important to know how to CREATE the customer, not just IDENTIFY them like everyone else is doing.



So if you’re not doing any of this type of stuff, then you’re largely leaving things to chance and you’re just another person in the game competing with everyone else doing the same thing.



And if you do the same thing as everyone else. Well, then, you get the same results as everyone else.



And what’s the point of being an average marketer? I don’t like that. I like to be better. I like to win.



So here’s how you win.



As I’ve said many times before, advertising, marketing and sales, at its core is nothing more than change work.



All we’re doing is changing opinions, feelings, thoughts, and such in the sales process in favor of the things we sell.



That’s the game we’re playing. From copywriters to Madison Avenue branding and PR firms, we are all playing the same game, and that game is one of change work.



Those of us who can enact the greatest change, also sell the most. It’s simple as that.



Since marketing is change work, the first question to ask is, how does change work begin? What generates it?



All change begins with a question.



I’ll say that louder for the people in the back of the group.



ALL CHANGE BEGINS WITH A QUESTION.



When you’re writing good copy, you’re basically generating questions in the prospect, and the answers are in your copy and your product, hence why they buy. That’s all you’re doing in the mind.



I like to use something called “assumptive questions” which I’ll  write about later in the week.



But for now, back to the topic at hand...quiz funnels.



The best way to go from identifying the customer to creating the customer in a quiz funnel is via specific questions that I like to call functional questions.



Aside from members of Fast Forward and NHB+, I’ve never seen anyone in this game use functional questions anywhere in the sales process. So you’ll be amongst the very few who do.



Since most quiz funnels merely identify a customer, the way we create customers is by going through this transformational arc:



1. We capture them in an information seeking state (ads, quiz landers)

2. We put them in a problem state using functional questions, as you’ll see in the rest of this post.

3. Once they’re through that, we move them to a solution state where they are presented with a solution

4. And then we pitch our product or service



So the ads that lead to the quiz should always have some type of pay off of discovery (information seeking).



For high emotional state markets, the word “HOW” works well, so an ad or headline that leads with:



DISCOVER HOW TO ______________



For low emotional state, the word “WHY” works well, so an ad or headline that leads with:



DISCOVER WHY TO ______________



Read my posts on high and low emotional states to learn how to modulate this here.



Now, this information that you are offering on the lander always should lead to an outcome (because people buy for outcomes, not features or benefits) and once they know the outcome, they will get what they want.



So we take them through the quiz funnel and as they go through the quiz funnel, they are asked to answer questions.



If we go from standard questions to functional questions, we can affect the emotional state to a great degree.



For example, if I have a question that states:



- How old are you?

        - 18-23

        - 24-29

        - 30-35

        - 36-40



This question doesn’t do anything, it’s just a standard information question.



Now, to make it a functional question, we make it look like this:



- This year, I’m...

        - This year, I’m between 18-23 years old and I have a great future ahead of me

        - This year, I’m between 24-29, and I’m feeling optimistic

        - This year, I’m between 30-35, and I”m feeling better than ever

        - This year, I’m between 36-40, and I can’t see what the future holds in store for me



Read the two and notice the amount of energy that’s generated between these two. One is A LOT more personal whereas one is just whatever, and also notice the “I am” in the question and the answer, this is what makes the question FUNCTION.



So, the beginning of the quiz should always be inviting and positive, the middle of the quiz neutral and the end of the quiz goes into negative.



The reason for the end always being negative in nature is because as you’ll see below that is a must for problem states and without problem states, you can’t get to solution states.



So think of it as 1/3 positive, 1/3 neutral and 1/3 negative.



Feel free to play with the ratios, but don’t down play the 3rd negative section, that will make or break the quiz.



We move them from one mind state to another mind state using functional questions.



Functional questions serve a function, and that function is that it makes them realize that they have a problem or implies that something is wrong.



Example: 



“I gaining weight because”



- I’m gaining weight because I can’t control my cravings

-  I’m gaining weight because I can’t stick to a diet

-  I’m gaining weight because I don’t have time to work out

-  I’m gaining weight because I don’t have the energy to focus on losing weight

-  I’m gaining weight because I don’t know what to do or change my situation

-  I’m gaining weight because everything I tried doesn’t work



See it? This assumes that they are gaining weight, and all choice force the agreement of that mind frame.



This takes the mind and takes it from feeling good, to feeling neutral to feeling not so great.



So what happens is they came in feeling bad (information seeking) via ads, the quiz made them feel good via positive questions.



Then the quiz goes in the mind, thru questions into feeling bad.



We feel bad when we question ourselves on an identity level.



Problem state in ad -> solution state on page -> positive, back to even more problematic.



Identify them. Identify the situation. Identify the problem. 



They have to agree to these things in order to move forward and get the outcome of the qiz.



Now, before they get to the quiz, the ad should entice them to discover their limitations that are preventing them from getting the outcome.



Because to go from where they are to where they want to be, there is a limitation in the middle, once they discover this limitation, that will allow them to move fwd.



Ads are them probing into their limitations.



Discover HOW or Discover WHY, or any type of variation of the headline.



Diagnostic marketing is the future. 



Start the quiz with positive questions or identifiers. These are external questions.



If you put identifiers 1st, the quiz take rate will be LOWER than if you put positive questions.



Positive questions tend to be “TOWARDS”, and the language of towards is:



- I want to

- I’d like to

- I wish to

- I choose to

- I hope to

- I dream to



These are disassociated so they don’t feel much, cuz it’s not about them, it’s about the thing they do.



For positive questions, you can use “YOU” instead of “I”, it also work very well.



Then we go to the middle of the quiz, which is the neutral question territory:



It’s about what they do, where they’re at etc…What’s going on in their lives.



Neutral language is:



- I tend to

- Sometimes I

- Once in a while I

- Etc...



Here you can also use “YOU” instead of “I”.



You can start using You in the 1st half of the neutral questions, and “I” on the second set.



The reason being is that You tends to disassociate, where as I associates more.



Once you use those set of questions, then it’s time to move to negative questions.



Negative questions tend to use AWAY language.



“You” questions on the front and neutral and “I am” questions on the back end.



“I am” questions and answers cause “negative ownership”.



In order to help them get out of the problem they have, we have to make them aware and accept the problem.



If someone is a smoker, we have to get them to accept the fact that smoking is bad.



The way this looks like is:



- “I have”

- “I have had this problem for 5 years, 10 years, 15 years, 20 years”

- “I am”

- “I am a little bit ashamed of my weight”

- “I am quite ashamed of my weight”

- “No matter what I do I can’t lose weight”



Take “I” and combine it with AWAY language and you have negative admittance which cause them to accept ownership of the problem.



These should be identities, so they are associated. Here we’re being asked to admit things we never want to admit



So the questions get them to ADMIT they have a problem on an identity level and at the same time admit that they can not get rid of it.



Once they go through this process. The curiosity has no become intent and there is a lot of emotion, and out of this they come to the sales page.



On the sales page, you can sell a product, pitch book a call, whatever you want.



Now that they’re on the sales page, the goal is to thank them for taking the quiz, build rapport and then insert some form of comfort language so that way they feel that you care about them. 



This is very important as they just came out of a set of very negative idenity building questions. 



Which you should as they are your customers :-)



Comfort language is all about NOT BEING ALONE. 



Example is “You’re not the only one who took this”, etc..



Then the sales page always opens with “here’s the bad news” or “here’s something you have to know” something that bounces off of that negative state in the last set of questions in the quiz to CONTINUE that feeling.



Where they go “oh what now!”, and then you go into it.



The reason we do this is to create a massive contrast to the good news that follows which will change everything for them.



Because, remember...CONTRAST is the basis of AWARENESS, and the ore contrast, the more they will be aware of our solution and product and it’s value to them.



You create a set of generalizations that bundle people up that they can identify with that are reflected as true from those questions.



The bundles have “negative labels”.



E.g. in the parenting niche - Your parenting style is “Authoritarian”



Parents with this style tend to ________ and because of this, your child will comply in the short term, but the real effects are going to be seen in a year or two as it does deep psychological damage to the child blah blah blah”



[you insert the bad news as to why they could not get the results based on the last part of the quiz]



Human beings tend to REMEMBER THE LAST THING THAT WAS SAID.



You explain the bad news, and this sets up and explains the reason why they have had the problem.



Then you reveal the MECHANISM OF THE PROBLEM ITSELF.



After that, the flip over is easy.



Which sets up the next section which is:



Here’s the good news:



Here’s how you fix it all -> from here you can sell, book a call, do whatever.



If you want to increase compliance, give them a reason why you’re asking.



That’s the gist of it, now some nuances...



You want to ask questions that elicit sensory experiences that bring about things that are deep down in their minds that they really do not want to think about let alone talk about.



Such as:



I struggle with ________



I have a hard time with __________



I find it impossible to __________



I can’t seem to over come ________



If women, add “FEEL”



I feel overwhelmed when ___________



Future pace the questions with NEGATIVE POSSIBILITY:



The #1 thing I’m afraid of is ______________



The reason we’re asking is we need to know how to best help you overcome that.



Here’s how it was done in the dating space to great effect:



- I’m afraid that I’ll be alone forever

- I’m afraid that I’ll meet the wrong guy and it won’t work out

- I’m afraid that I’ll keep having to use dating apps well into my 40s

- I’m afraid that I’ll be the only one in my social circle who’s still single



[That’s what I’m the most afraid of, let’s continue]



Here’s an example in the coaching space:



Because there are so many coaches coming into the market, I’m very concerned that:



The reason we’re asking is we need to know how to best help you overcome that.



1. I won’t be able to get clients

2. My clients will leave me for other coaches

3. I will become irrelevant if I can’t generate new clients



On the negative “probe” give them an out in allowing them to say “Something else” if clicked they type it on or you can allow them to skip it.



Just the act of reading is sometimes enough to bring the problem to light.



This isolates attention by isolating them in their own attention.



What’s really interesting about all this is that if you get someone to admit they have a problem, by default you are also getting them to admit that they need help, and you are the person who can help them.



Here’s some examples of what we’ve done in Fast Forward using quizzes:



Survival quiz



- how much longer could you survive when disaster strikes?

- How safe is your location

- A secret test to test if you can trust your neighbors

- Is your plan going to work?

- The perfect plan for you and your family



Parenting



- Parenting market. You can add in how many days left of their childhood they have left. So you show where they are, and how much they have left.

- There was a study I forgot the name but it said by the time you turn 18, you’ve spent 90% of the time you’ll ever spend with your parents



Dating



- What’s your dating style?

- Are you secretly repelling high value men?

- The highest caliber women that would consider dating you

- Are you dateable?



Weight Loss



- How fast can you lose weight

- Discover what hormones are preventing you from losing weight



Biz-Op



- How much are your skills worth?

- How long would it take you to make a million dollars?

- How fast can you launch you own business and make $10k?



Quit Smoking



- How addicted you are to smoking and how long it’ll take you to quit



DFY Ecom 

- How much can your ecom store idea make

- How fast can you store scale



 Golfing

- How good can you get? 

- How much can you add to your drive

 

Muscle Building

- How much muscle can your body carry without TREN?

- Discover your natural genetic potential

So on and so forth, and I’ll leave you with a golden nugget of this post.

If you want to amp up the quiz to generate even more intent, after your set of negative questions, a pressure-release question is this:


“What would you like instead?”



- Choice 1

- Choice 2

- Choice 3

- Choice 4

This frames that they have choice, once they feel they have choice. They are a lot more apt to act.

The choice they choose would go to the page that has that solution, so this question directs them to a uniquely personal sales page.

So there you have it, a long post that’s long over due.

As always, if you have any questions, comments, answers or whatever — just hit the comment box below and let me know.

Oh and if you would, smash the like and love button for the algorithm so everyone in NHB can see it.


To your success,


Alen Sultanic

column1: Boost AOV by giving more buying options
column2:
Self Sorting Upsells: How to get an instant 15-25% AOV boost by using Self Sorting Upsells 

I consider the upsell path to be the most important contributing factor (next to the front end copy) when it comes to scaling an offer on cold traffic...

...Because it has the highest price elasticity in your sales process (since the customer is in a massive buying state) - which allows it to become the highest AOV contribution to your funnel...

...Meaning if your front end offer is priced at $50 and your upsell 1 is $200 with a conversion rate of 25% then you now have an additional $50 added to the AOV.

...Easily doubling the value the front end brings.

Now, let's talk about how to kick this up and make it even better.



Just about everyone in the industry uses a "funnelcentric" model, meaning...you put people at the top and you filter them down the funnel...100 buy the front end, 25 buy upsell one, 15 buy upsell two and so on...until the end of the funnel.



Nothing wrong with that...it works and gets the job done...



...Except for the fact that it's based on a filtering model that's based in exclusion.



It filters out A LOT of people out of the funnel - solely based on price.



So the typical upsell path goes something like this:



Front end offer: $50



Upsell 1: $200



Upsell 1 Downsell: 2 payments of $100 



Upsell 2: $150



Upsell 2 Downsell: 2 payments of $75



You get the idea...



...Now here's the problem...remember I just said that the funnelcentric model is based on filtering and exclusion?



For every single person who buys upsell 1 at $200...you got a whole lot of people who would have gladly bought it at $50, $100, $150, $250, $300, and even $500...



...But because of the funnelcentric model of filtering and exclusion that money is not captured, leaving a whole lot of waste in the process.



So what do you do?



You crack open a beer, take a sip and go "holy shit" to what I'm about to show you...



...What we do is use self sorting upsells and they work like this:



Front end: $50



Upsell 1 option A: $50 / month



Upsell 1 option B: $150 for X



Upsell 1 option c: $197 for a lifetime



NOW...you just gave the person a chance who wants to pay $197 to pay $197...



...the guy or girl who'd go for the $50 / month is gonna take that and the person in the middle who was comfortable with $150 will pay that.



They are SELF SORTING and you're using INCLUSION.



This is normally done in the supplement space with the 1,3, 6 bottle plan...



...But NEVER EVER done in the information marketing space - until now.



Here's how I'm doing it in one of my book funnels:



Front end: $5



Upsell 1 option A: $49 one site license



Upsell 1 option B: $97 three site license



Upsell 1 option C: $197 unlimited site license



And so on...you get the idea.



I shared this with Jay Deiboldt about 2-3 months ago and they used it on their funnel and it's been of the secret weapons they've used to scale and hold scale at 1,000+ sales a day on YouTube.



They tested the 3 options of self sorting upsells and it boosted the AOV then they went to 2 options for self sorting upsells and that won, here is their data:



3 Options added $13 extra to the AOV



2 Options added $19 extra on AOV



Their model is:



Upsell 1 Option A: $49 / month



Upsell 1 Option B: $149 / lifetime access



End result: $61,000 in recurring a month from the $49 a month



75% take the $149



25% 49 a month option



Previous funnelcentric inclusion based upsell was:



$97 X 2 = control - 



The outcome:



It converts the exactly the same but way more money...instead of 75% of ppl getting the $97 2 pay, you get $147 in two pay..



$97 x 2 = total collected $135 after 30 days (remaining later)



same exact conversion rate of the upsell



75% of the people are paying $149 day 0



25% buy the $47 a month plan 



Anything after that 1st rebill = pure profit



Every single person I shared this with has boosted their AOV significantly. 



Let me know if you have any questions on how to implement self sorting upsells in your funnel and I'll gladly answer them in the comments.



Oh and one more thing...this 100% applies to the back end as well...ALLOW people to self sort based on price.

Post Edit: Jay Deiboldt just shared with us what the self sort upsells look like in his path.

column1: How to boost conversion rates by 30%
column2:
When you ask them a question on your book-a-call forms, tell them why you're asking them that question.

You'll increase your conversion rate of the book-a-call form by about 20-30%.

Example:

What's the reason you're looking to (get outcome)

We're asking you because _____________
We're asking you so we can ____________
We're asking you, so we know how to _____________

Or add, "The reason we're asking is," etc...

Having any reason will boost conversions.

Having good reasons will boost it even more and make them feel that you're really trying to help them out by asking them those questions.

The psychology behind it is that if you have a stranger ask you questions, your subconscious automatically goes into self-defense mode to "Why are you asking me this?" and starts looking as to what they're trying to get from you. If you explain why you're asking, it's no longer in self-defense mode because it flips it and is now GIVING something to them.

- Alen

p.s. Tested this in women's dating, men's dating, weight loss, trading, crypto, and other niches, and worked in every niche tested.

To everyone creating, building or running low-ticket offers that wants to easily increase conversion rates...
Have had people in FF and NHB+ do this for about 18 months now and realized I never shared it here. One of those "elusive obvious" moments.

Step 1: Go here https://placeit.net/?search=reading+book and find people that look like your target audience.

Step 2: Upload your book cover and generate images of your book or course.

Step 3: Sprinkle images of your book being read / enjoyed / consumed throughout your sales copy, ads and emails.

Step 4: Watch converison go up.

That's all for tonight.

column1: Upsells based on ZIP Codes and gender

column2:
How To Write A Supplement VSL That Converts

The 20 Most Important Things I’ve Learned From Writing 20 Different Supplement VSL’s That Have Generated 8 Figures + For ONE Company Over The Last 18 Months

Part 2:  Blood Pressure

Writing a VSL to sell a blood pressure supplement is a serious business.

Because as you know high blood pressure is linked to heart attacks which is one of the biggest killers in the world.

So of course the great motivator here is FEAR.

Which is why you may have seen all those Clickbank VSL’s starting off with all those nightmare stories with people passing out, having heart attacks while driving and all kinds of madness.

Because THAT’S what works.

And the way I see it…

If that’s what it takes to wake people up about their blood pressure issues then I’m ALL for it.

Because if I have to choose between someone getting SCARED at a VSL and BUYING something that will help them…

Or, being scared while having a heart attack because they didn't buy. I know which one I’d choose. Anyway..

So,  in the opening of your blood pressure VSL fear works best.

Now depending on what platform your VSL is running on you might run into compliance issues when trying to use a nightmare story opening.

And with some really strict platforms like FB – and even clickbank sometimes…

They won’t even let you mention the word “Heart Attack'  in the same VSL script when you’re selling a supplement.

Fortunately though, there IS a way around this which I’ll share with you.

But first let’s look at how to write a ‘not super strict compliant’ blood pressure VSL that converts.  And THEN I’ll show the ‘super compliant way’  so you have the best of both.

So, the first thing as we’ve covered is to open your VSL with a nightmare story.  The way we did it was to tell the real life story of my client's fathers heart shutting down and having to be airlifted to the hospital…

With the doctors having no idea what caused his heart attack.  Which led to my Doctor client having to help his Dad heal by himself.

After that, you then want to highlight the NEW causes for high blood pressure you’ve discovered that your prospect might not have heard of yet.

In our case we used:

*Constricted blood vessels.

*Out of control Adrenaline levels.

*Super high inflammation.

*And high homocysteine levels – which causes cholesterol to become “sticky,” or oxidized, which can damage arteries and increase blood vessel blockages in the heart, brain, and other organs.

After educating about these new causes and showing proof…

You then get to the point where you want to introduce your ingredients / herbs / solution.

And here is where I see a lot of copywriters drop the ball.

And write something like:

“So I did a lot of research and found these ingredients that help with these (new problems you’ve mentioned) here’s a bunch of studies to prove it..”

Now, logically I know it makes sense to do this.  But it’s actually the HARDEST way to get someone to believe your solution works.

A far more effective way is to introduce CONTRAST before you talk about your solution.

This is something we did in our blood pressure VSL by unknowingly.

But I’ve since learned WHY this works from Alen.

Without wanting to reveal any high level NHB+ / Fast Forward level stuff here…

Basically,  before mentioning your ingredients you want to talk about a place in the world…

Or a group of people…

Or a situation where this problem either doesn’t exist or is greatly reduced.

As what this does is OPEN the mind up to new possibilities.

And helps removes scepticism before you start getting into details about your ingredients.

Because when you don’t introduce contrast BEFORE mentioning your product or ingredients, people will wonder…

“If this is so good, why haven’t I heard of this before, right?

So what we did in our VSL was talk about how people in Japan have extremely low cases of heart disease and blood pressure…

Despite the fact that they drink FOUR times more alcohol … eat loads of red meat…  and smoke more than people in the Western world.

And explained that the reason why they get away with it, is because they have been using a herbal treatment system called Kampo.

Which everyone takes over there.  Which includes xyz herbs. Which is what we’ve now custom made into our supplement. Here’s the proof. And here’s how to get it.

So to sum up:

The two most important things for blood pressure VSLs..

1.	Fear based opening. (where possible)

2.	Introduce the CONTRAST (People / Place / Situation where this problem doesn't exist or greatly reduced) before talking about your solution to enhance believability.

Jason
